key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can simultaneously predict laser welding penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from weld-pool video and process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ weld quality control, helping operators detect incomplete penetration and morphology defects before destructive inspection.
method: Uses CMOS camera top-weld-pool images as the primary sensing signal.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state-space models to capture spatial and temporal features.
Fuses image features with welding parameters in a multi-task prediction framework.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on camera quality, process consistency, and the representativeness of the constructed dataset when transferring to different welding setups or materials.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
